data.title: Southside Bancshares, Inc. (NYSE:SBSI) declares $0.36 per share cash dividend payable on Sept 4 to shareholders on record by Aug 21.
data.description: Southside Bancshares, Inc., based in Tyler, Texas, has announced that its Board of Directors has approved a regular quarterly cash dividend for its shareholders. The parent company of Southside Bank, listed on the NYSE as SBSI, will be issuing a dividend of $0.36 per common share. This cash dividend is set to be paid out on September 4, 2025, to shareholders of record as of August 21, 2025.

data.title: KBR appoints Huibert Vigeveno to its board of directors on August 5, 2025. NYSE: KBR.
data.description: Houston-based company KBR (NYSE: KBR) has recently revealed the addition of Huibert H. Vigeveno to its board of directors as of August 5, 2025. Vigeveno officially joined the board on that date, bringing his expertise and insights to the company.

data.title: Microchip Technology declares quarterly cash dividend of 45.5 cents/share, payable to stockholders on September 5, 2025.
data.description: In Chandler, Arizona, on August 7, 2025, Microchip Technology Incorporated (NASDAQ: MCHP) revealed a quarterly cash dividend of 45.5 cents per share on its common stock. The dividend will be distributed on September 5, 2025, to stockholders recorded on August 22, 2025. Microchip began issuing quarterly cash dividends in Q3 of FY 2003.

data.title: Bioventus (Nasdaq: BVS) CEO to speak at Canaccord Genuity 45th Annual Global Growth Conference on August 12, 2025 at 2:00 p.m. ET.
data.description: Bioventus Inc., a prominent company specializing in innovations for active healing, disclosed that its President and CEO, Rob Claypoole, is set to engage in a fireside chat during the Canaccord Genuity Global Growth Conference on August 12, 2025. This event is scheduled for 2:00 p.m. Eastern Time. Bioventus is listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "BVS". This news was shared on August 7, 2025, from Durham, North Carolina.

data.title: Jenny Martinez introduces Mesa Mia, a new food brand inspired by Latin home cooking, in partnership with Xcel Brands and TSC Product Lab.
data.description: Jenny Martinez introduces Mesa Mia, a dynamic food brand influenced by traditional Latin home cooking. This culinary venture is a collaboration with Xcel Brands and TSC Product Lab.

data.title: Critical One Energy Inc. finalizes uranium properties sale to Dark Star Minerals Inc., announced on August 7, 2025.
data.description: On August 7, 2025, in Toronto, Critical One Energy Inc. (formerly Madison Metals Inc.), known as Critical One, revealed the completion of its uranium asset sale to Dark Star Minerals Inc., as disclosed on June 12, 2025. Under this deal, Critical One transferred its assets to Dark Star for an undisclosed amount. Critical One's stock tickers are CSE: CRTL, OTCQB: MMTLF, and FSE: 4EF0, while Dark Star trades under CSE: BATT and FSE: P0W. This move marks a significant step for both companies in the energy sector.
Alta Equipment Group releases Q2 2025 financial results with impressive numbers.

data.title: ARRAY Technologies, Inc. reports 84% year-to-date volume growth over 2024 in Q2 financial results.
data.description: Business success remains strong with year-to-date volume increasing by 84% compared to 2024. This growth is attributed to continued commercial excellence in operations.

data.title: Bitcoin reclaims $117,000 as Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin rise. Daily active addresses up 7.8%. 96,836 traders liquidated in 24 hours for $285.70M.
data.description: Cryptocurrency markets are up, with Bitcoin at $117,325.42. President Trump's proposal to include crypto assets in 401(k) plans is fueling optimism. Notable price data: Ethereum is $3,861.97, Solana is at $171.35, XRP is $3.08, Dogecoin is $0.2168, and Shiba Inu is $0.00001263. Transaction data: Bitcoin's large transactions rose by 3.6% while Ethereum's dropped by 6.5%. Daily active addresses increased for Bitcoin by 7.8% and decreased for Ethereum by 3.4%. 96,836 traders were liquidated, totaling $285.70 million. Top gainers include Mantle, Pendle, and Conflux. Other developments include Transak adding support for USDe and ProShares introducing a leveraged ETF targeting Circle.
Monster Beverage announces second quarter financial results for 2025.

data.title: E-L Financial announces $23.29 net equity per common share on June 30, 2025, with $365M Q2 net income & $451M YTD net income in 2025. Share split declared on May 7, 2025. E-L Corporate reports $333M Q2 net income & $349M YTD. Empire Life reports $32M Q2 net income & $102M YTD.
data.description: E-L Financial Corporation Limited reported a net equity value per common share of $23.29 as of June 30, 2025, a 0.3% decrease from December 31, 2024, and a 9.7% increase from June 30, 2024. For the second quarter of 2025, the company reported a consolidated shareholder's net income of $365 million, compared to $232 million in 2024, and a net income of $451 million for the first six months of 2025, compared to $779 million in 2024. E-L Financial approved a 100 for one share split on May 7, 2025. E-L Corporate reported a net income of $333 million for the second quarter of 2025 compared to $197 million in 2024. Empire Life reported a net income of $32 million for the second quarter of 2025 and $102 million for the first six months of 2025. Empire Life's Life Insurance Capital Adequacy Test total ratio was 142% at June 30, 2025, compared to 151% at December 31, 2024.

data.title: Genpact's Q2 2025 saw net revenues hit $1.254 billion, up 6.6% YoY. Data-Tech-AI net revenues rose 9.7%. Diluted EPS was $0.75, up 11.9%.
data.description: Genpact Limited announced its financial results for the second quarter of 2025 with net revenues of $1.254 billion, a 6.6% increase year-over-year. Data-Tech-AI net revenues were $599 million, up 9.7%; Digital Operations net revenues were $655 million, up 4.0%. Advanced Technology Solutions net revenues were $293 million, up 17.3%, and Core Business Services net revenues were $962 million, up 3.8%. Diluted EPS was $0.75, an 11.9% increase, and adjusted diluted EPS was $0.88, up 11.4%. The company repurchased 700,000 shares for $30 million. For the third quarter, they forecast net revenues of $1.258-$1.270 billion. For the full year, the updated outlook includes net revenues of $4.958-$5.053 billion and adjusted diluted EPS of $3.51-$3.58. Join the earnings call on August 7, 2025. Genpact aims for accelerated growth in AI-driven transformation for clients.

data.title: Lionsgate reports $556M revenue, $94M net loss from continuing operations in Q1 fiscal 2026; 12% growth in 12-month library revenue to $989M.
data.description: Lionsgate Studios Corp reported first-quarter results with studio revenue reaching $556 million. The net loss from continuing operations attributable to shareholders was $94.0 million, with an adjusted net loss of $88.1 million. The operating loss was $10.6 million, and Adjusted OIBDA showed a loss of $3.7 million. Trailing 12-month library revenue grew by 12% to a record $989 million. In the Motion Picture segment, revenue decreased to $267.3 million, while in the Television Production segment, revenue increased to $288.5 million. Lionsgate plans to return to solid growth in the upcoming fiscal year by releasing major film tentpoles and doubling scripted television series deliveries. Lionsgate's Senior Management discussed these results in a call on August 7th, 2025. Liongate's consolidated balance sheets showed assets totaling $5,160.9 million, liabilities at $6,164.0 million, and equity at a deficit of $1,118.0 million. The consolidated statements of operations displayed operating revenue of $525.9 million and an operating loss of $10.6 million. The consolidated statements of cash flows highlighted an operating net loss of $91.7 million. Lionsgate Studios Corp remains optimistic about future growth despite current challenges.

data.title: Primaris REIT declares $0.0717 per unit distribution for August 2025, $0.86 annualized. Payable on Sept 15 to unitholders.
data.description: In Toronto, Primaris Real Estate Investment Trust (TSX: PMZ.UN) disclosed a distribution of $0.0717 per unit for August 2025, equaling $0.86 annually. The payment will be made on September 15, 2025, to unitholders on record as of August 29, 2025. Primaris is Canada's sole enclosed shopping center-focused REIT.

data.title: Clipper Realty Inc. reports Q2 2025 financial results: $39.0 million in revenue, up 5% from last year, and $10.1 million in income from operations.
data.description: In New York, Clipper Realty Inc. (NYSE: CLPR) disclosed its financial and operating performance for the quarter ending June 30, 2025. As a prominent owner and operator of multifamily residential and commercial properties in the New York metropolitan area, the company reported quarterly revenues reaching $39.0 million, reflecting a 5% increase compared to the previous year. Additionally, quarterly income from operations amounted to $10.1 million for the same period.

White House: Trump to make announcement on the economy.

data.title: F&M Bank opens second full-service office in Michigan, located in Troy. This adds to their existing presence in the state.
data.description: Today, F&M Bank unveiled the launch of its second complete service branch in Michigan. Situated in Troy, Michigan, the new office further expands the bank's presence in the region, offering comprehensive banking services to customers.

data.title: Stocks close mixed as S&P 500 dips 0.1%, a minimal change from initial tariff uproar in April.
data.description: The S&P 500 closed down by 0.1% at the end of the day, marking a relatively minor drop compared to the initial announcement of tariffs in early April.

data.title: Nerdy Inc. reports strong Q2 2025 results, revenue growth and beat EBITDA guidance, aiming for profitability in Q4.
data.description: Nerdy Inc. (NYSE: NRDY) revealed its financial performance for the second quarter ended June 30, 2025, in St. Louis. Chuck Cohn, the Founder, Chairman, and CEO of Nerdy, stated that the Live+AI™ strategy was instrumental in driving growth in Q2. The company witnessed a resurgence in Learning Membership revenue and exceeded EBITDA expectations. Additionally, the introduction of AI-native tools aimed at empowering learners and educators with real-time capabilities is underway. These developments have positioned Nerdy on a path towards profitability by Q4. For more information, kindly refer to the Nerdy invest.
